title: APIv2: ping endpoint is 404
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`libpod/_ping` endpoint does not seem to be correctly wired up.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
`sudo curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.24/libpod/_ping` returns `Not Found`.
Also tried `libpod/ping`, `ping`, `_ping` but all are 404. According to [doc]( http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodPingGet) it should be `libpod/_ping`.

comments.body:
I am on PTO here but just a quick peek suggests these are all under non-versioned paths.

```
curl  --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/_ping'
curl  --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/libpod/_ping
```

@marusak I need some more of your help.  `_ping` is currently always unversioned as the returned headers are how the client determines if it is compatible with the server.
```
$ curl  --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/_ping'
$ curl  --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/libpod/_ping
$ curl  -XHEAD --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/_ping'
$ curl  -XHEAD --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/libpod/_ping
```
All above work as expected for me.  These endpoints are meant to be compatible with other systems so I cannot change them. Two items:
1) Do the above commands work as expected for you?
2) Would additional details in the documentation have prevented this confusion?

Ah thanks, I totally not guessed it would be unversioned.
The documentation does not mention this in any way and it it utterly confusing.

> Do the above commands work as expected for you?

Yes it does.

> Would additional details in the documentation have prevented this confusion?

Absolutely. It should be obvious how to use this endpoint, especially since it differs from other ones.
